description: Iran's Ministry of Justice (Vezarat-e Dadgostari) manages the administrative interface between the executive and judiciary. The minister does not head the Judiciary — the Chief Justice is separately appointed by the Supreme Leader — but administers courts, prisons, and judicial coordination. Key ministers: [Morteza Bakhtiari](/people/morteza-bakhtiari) (2009–2013, EU-sanctioned, alleged 1988 Death Commission member), [Mostafa Pourmohammadi](/people/mostafa-pourmohammadi) (2013–2017, implicated in 1988 massacre as deputy minister), Alireza Avaei (2017–2021, sanctioned for executions), [Amin Hossein Rahimi](/people/amin-hossein-rahimi) (2021–2024), Amin Hossein Rahimi (2024–present, reappointed by Pezeshkian).
editorial_notes: Note: The Ministry of Justice is a cabinet portfolio; the head of the Judiciary is separately appointed by the Supreme Leader and is NOT a cabinet minister.
